issue_summary: Implicit argument conversion for UUID, URL, BigDecimal, BigInteger, File​
javadoc_before:
/** * {@code DefaultArgumentConverter} is the default implementation of the * {@link ArgumentConverter} API. * *

The {@code DefaultArgumentConverter} is able to convert from strings to a * number of primitive types and their corresponding wrapper types (Byte, Short, * Integer, Long, Float, and Double), date and time types from the * {@code java.time} package, and some miscellaneous types * ({@link java.util.Currency}, {@link java.util.Locale}, and * {@link java.net.URI}). * *

If the source and target types are identical the source object will not * be modified. * * @since 5.0 * @see org.junit.jupiter.params.converter.ArgumentConverter */
javadoc_after:
/** * {@code DefaultArgumentConverter} is the default implementation of the * {@link ArgumentConverter} API. * *

The {@code DefaultArgumentConverter} is able to convert from strings to a * number of primitive types and their corresponding wrapper types (Byte, Short, * Integer, Long, Float, and Double), date and time types from the * {@code java.time} package, and some additional common Java types such as * {@link File}, {@link BigDecimal}, {@link BigInteger}, {@link Currency}, * {@link Locale}, {@link URI}, {@link URL}, {@link UUID}, etc. * *

If the source and target types are identical the source object will not * be modified. * * @since 5.0 * @see org.junit.jupiter.params.converter.ArgumentConverter */
